---
title: "Analysis"
author: "Sunduss Hamdan and Kaela Girod"
format: html
embed-resources: true
execute:
  warning: false
  message: false
editor: visual
editor_options: 
  chunk_output_type: console
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(tidymodels)
library(mclust)
library(tidyclust)
library(factoextra)
library(broom)
library(sf)
library(h2o)
```

```{r}

## Load Data and Prepare Modeling Data

nyc_final <- st_read("data/nyc_final.shp")

# [I think we need to do some exploratory data analysis, here and in the writeup]
# [since it's part of our analysis process]

# drop geometry before modeling
nyc_data <- nyc_final |>
  st_drop_geometry()

# check for highly correlated predictors [do not assign this to anything]
# [i've added a recipe step that removes the ID vars for PCA but we need them to
# identify tracts at the end]
# [also need to figure out the best way to format this table if we use it]
nyc_data |>
  select(-GEOID, -NAME, -contyfp) |>
  cor(use = "complete.obs") |>
  round(2)

sum(!complete.cases(nyc_data))
```

## Generalized Low Rank Modeling (GLRM)
```{r}
h2o.no_progress()  # turn off progress bars
h2o.init(max_mem_size = "5g")  # connect to H2O instance

# convert data to h2o object
my_basket.h2o <- as.h2o(my_basket)

# run basic GLRM
basic_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = my_basket.h2o,
  k = 20, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 123
)

# get top level summary information on our model
summary(basic_glrm)

plot(basic_glrm)

# amount of variance explained by each archetype (aka "pc")
basic_glrm@model$importance

data.frame(
    PC  = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% seq_along(),
    PVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[2,] %>% unlist(),
    CVE = basic_glrm@model$importance %>% .[3,] %>% unlist()
) %>%
    gather(metric, variance_explained, -PC) %>%
    ggplot(aes(PC, variance_explained)) +
    geom_point() +
    facet_wrap(~ metric, ncol = 1, scales = "free")

t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes)[1:5, 1:5]


p1 <- t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  mutate(feature = row.names(.)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Arch1, reorder(feature, Arch1))) +
  geom_point()

p2 <- t(basic_glrm@model$archetypes) %>% 
  as.data.frame() %>% 
  mutate(feature = row.names(.)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(Arch1, Arch2, label = feature)) +
  geom_text()

gridExtra::grid.arrange(p1, p2, nrow = 1)


# Re-run model with k = 8
k8_glrm <- h2o.glrm(
  training_frame = my_basket.h2o,
  k = 8, 
  loss = "Quadratic",
  regularization_x = "None", 
  regularization_y = "None", 
  transform = "STANDARDIZE", 
  max_iterations = 2000,
  seed = 123
)

# Reconstruct to see how well the model did
my_reconstruction <- h2o.reconstruct(k8_glrm, my_basket.h2o, reverse_transform = TRUE)

# Raw predicted values
my_reconstruction[1:5, 1:5]

# Round values to whole integers
my_reconstruction[1:5, 1:5] %>% round(0)

```

## Gaussian Mixture Modeling (GMM)
```{r}
library(mclust)

gmm_fit <- Mclust(glrm_repr)

clusters <- gmm_fit$classification

glrm_results <- nyc_data |>
  sf::st_drop_geometry() |>
  dplyr::select(GEOID) |>
```


## Principle Component Analysis (PCA)
```{r}

# normalize and run PCA across all components first
# so we can inspect variance explained before deciding how many to keep
# [might want to dummy out the county variable, since it's only 5 counties and 
# we might want to keep some census tracts together based on their county]
pca_rec <- nyc_data |>
  recipe() |>
  add_role(GEOID, NAME, contyfp, new_role = "id") |>
  step_rm(has_role("id")) |>
  step_indicate_na(all_predictors) |>
  step_impute_mean(all_predictors) |>
  step_normalize(all_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_predictors(), id = "pca")

pca_rec_fit <- pca_rec |>
  prep()

# how much variance does each component explain?
tidy(pca_rec_fit, id = "pca", type = "variance")

# what does each component capture?
tidy(pca_rec_fit, id = "pca", type = "coef")

# scree plot — look for the elbow to decide how many components to keep
tidy(pca_rec_fit, id = "pca", type = "variance") |>
  filter(terms == "percent variance") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = component, y = value)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  labs(title = "Scree Plot",
    x = "Principal Component",
    y = "Percent of Variance Explained") +
  theme_minimal()

# [EDIT — replace num_comp once you have inspected the scree plot]
pca_rec_final <- nyc_vars |>
  recipe() |>
  step_normalize(all_predictors()) |>
  step_pca(all_predictors(), num_comp = 5, id = "pca")

```

## K-Means Clustering
```{r}

# set seed because k-means is not deterministic
set.seed(20200205)

# set up 5-fold cross validation
nyc_cv <- vfold_cv(nyc_vars, v = 5)

# k-means spec with num_clusters = tune()
kmeans_spec <- k_means(
  num_clusters = tune()) |>
  set_engine("stats", nstart = 100)

# workflow combining pca recipe and k-means spec
kmeans_wflow <- workflow(
  preprocessor = pca_rec_final,
  spec = kmeans_spec)

# tuning grid considering 1 to 10 clusters
clust_num_grid <- grid_regular(
  num_clusters(),
  levels = 10)

# tune the number of clusters
res <- tune_cluster(
  kmeans_wflow,
  resamples = nyc_cv,
  grid = clust_num_grid,
  control = control_grid(save_pred = TRUE, extract = identity),
  metrics = cluster_metric_set(sse_within_total, silhouette_avg))

# collect metrics
res_metrics <- res |>
  collect_metrics()

# elbow plot
res_metrics |>
  filter(.metric == "sse_within_total") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(x = "Number of clusters", y = "mean WSS over 5 folds") +
  theme_minimal()

# silhouette plot
res_metrics %>%
  filter(.metric == "silhouette_avg") |>
  ggplot(aes(x = num_clusters, y = mean)) +
  geom_point() +
  geom_line() +
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10) +
  labs(x = "Number of clusters", y = "avg silhouette width over 5 folds") +
  theme_minimal()

# gap statistic
nyc_vars_scaled <- pca_rec_final |>
  prep() |>
  bake(new_data = nyc_vars)

fviz_nbclust(nyc_vars_scaled, FUN = kmeans, method = "gap_stat")

# K means final model 


# [EDIT — replace num_clusters with chosen K from the plots above]
kmeans_spec_final <- k_means(
  num_clusters = 5) |>
  set_engine("stats", nstart = 100)

kmeans_wflow_final <- workflow(
  preprocessor = pca_rec_final,
  spec = kmeans_spec_final)

set.seed(20200205)

final_fit <- kmeans_wflow_final |>
  fit(data = nyc_vars)

# view cluster means
tidy(final_fit)

# combine results 
nyc_clusters <- bind_cols(
  select(nyc_model_data, GEOID, NAME),
  final_fit |>
    extract_recipe() |>
    bake(nyc_vars),
  cluster = final_fit |>
    extract_cluster_assignment() |>
    pull(.cluster))
```
